benchmark_metadata.title: Paired-end RNA-seq workflow execution in Galaxy
benchmark_metadata.benchmark_goal: Measure whether an agent can infer, plan, and execute an appropriate Galaxy RNA-seq workflow for the same real-world task when the user prompt is low-context, medium-context, or high-context.

canonical_task_interpretation.expected_execution_scope: all execution steps after planning occur in Galaxy only

low_context: Infer that the task is paired-end RNA-seq analysis from raw FASTQ inputs.
Choose a suitable Galaxy RNA-seq workflow path.
Report the correct analysis type, key artifact, and workflow step count.
medium_context: Respect the stated RNA-seq processing stages and outputs.
Use suitable Galaxy workflow or training resources for the paired-end RNA-seq task.
Report the correct analysis type, key artifact, and workflow step count.
high_context: Follow the explicitly requested RNA-seq processing pattern in Galaxy.
Execute a workflow consistent with fastp, STAR, quantification, and reporting stages.
Report the correct analysis type, key artifact, and workflow step count.

deterministic_checks.description: Execution after planning occurred only in Galaxy.
The run record shows Galaxy-native planning resources were checked.
The reported analysis type is RNA-seq.
The reported artifact is MultiQC HTML Report (dataset:html, state=ok).
The reported number of workflow tool steps is 16.

benchmark_gaps_covered: end-to-end execution of multi-stage RNA-seq analyses
